summary: DHH posts a brief job listing seeking a Ruby on Rails developer in the San Francisco Bay Area for a new startup. The unnamed company is beginning development with Rails and looking for local talent. DHH offers to connect interested developers with the firm. The company claims prior success and describes their new venture as exciting with wide appeal.
key_points: Early evidence of Rails adoption by Bay Area startups, just months after Rails' initial release
DHH personally brokering connections between Rails developers and companies, reflecting the tiny early community
The post signals Rails was gaining traction in the startup world before it was widely known
money_quotes: They bring prior "smash hit" experience to the table and are describing their new venture as "...very exciting and should have wide appeal".
key_insight: In Rails' earliest days, the community was so small that DHH personally acted as a matchmaker between the few developers and the first companies betting on the framework.
